reasoning: The Philadelphia Eagles are the defending Super Bowl 59 champions and opened as the consensus favorites (+600 implied ~14.3% with vig, ~12-13% true odds) to win Super Bowl 60. As of early November 2025, they boast a strong 6-2 record following a Week 9 bye. With Jalen Hurts and Saquon Barkley performing well, they remain grouped among the dominant tier of contenders alongside the Chiefs, Bills, and Lions. Because their 6-2 record aligns perfectly with preseason expectations of them being an elite contender, and they are largely healthy and performing at a high level entering Week 10, their true probability of winning the Super Bowl should remain very close to their opening odds. Given that some lower-tier teams have fallen out of contention by mid-season, their probability remains firmly crystallized around the 13-14% mark.
